api/ClaySharp.Interop.ClayNative.html.title: Class ClayNative | BlackHook
api/ClaySharp.Interop.ClayNative.html.summary: Class ClayNative Namespace ClaySharp.Interop Assembly BlackHook.ClaySharp.dll Provides low-level, high-performance C# bindings to the native Clay layout library. Uses source-generated marshalling via LibraryImportAttribute and compiles with runtime marshalling disabled for zero-overhead, direct native transitions. public static class ClayNative Inheritance object ClayNative Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Clay is a single-header C library for high-performance 2D layout. This class provides the direct interop wrapper. Since runtime marshalling is disabled, all parameters and return types are marshaled at compile-time by the C# compiler. Unsafe pointers are utilized heavily; developers must ensure correct lifetime management of memory backings and arenas. Methods BeginLayout() Begins a new layout pass. Must be called every frame before declaring UI elements. public static extern void BeginLayout() Clay_Initialize(Arena, Dimensions, ErrorHandler) Initializes the native Clay layout engine context with a memory arena, initial window dimensions, and an error handler. public static extern Context* Clay_Initialize(Arena arena, Dimensions layoutDimensions, ErrorHandler errorHandler) Parameters arena Arena The allocated memory arena for Clay's internal structures. layoutDimensions Dimensions The initial width and height of the layout viewport. errorHandler ErrorHandler The error handler callback and user data to receive error notifications. Returns Context* A raw pointer to the initialized internal Context structure. CloseElement() Low-level function: closes the current active layout element scope. public static extern void CloseElement() ConfigureOpenElement(ElementDeclaration) Low-level function: configures the currently open layout element with the provided styling declarations. public static extern void ConfigureOpenElement(ElementDeclaration config) Parameters config ElementDeclaration The configuration parameters for the element. ConfigureOpenElementPtr(in ElementDeclaration) Low-level function: configures the currently open layout element passing a reference to avoid structure copy. public static void ConfigureOpenElementPtr(in ElementDeclaration config) Parameters config ElementDeclaration A reference to the configuration parameters. CreateArenaWithCapacityAndMemory(nuint, void*) Creates a memory allocation arena using a pre-allocated block of raw unmanaged memory. public static extern Arena CreateArenaWithCapacityAndMemory(nuint capacity, void* memory) Parameters capacity nuint The capacity of the memory backing in bytes. Usually obtained via MinMemorySize(). memory void* A raw pointer to the block of unmanaged memory allocated for Clay. Returns Arena A initialized Arena struct ready to be passed to initialization. EndLayout() Concludes the current layout pass, computes alignments, sizes, and positions, and returns the resulting rendering commands. public static extern RenderCommandArray EndLayout() Returns RenderCommandArray A RenderCommandArray containing the drawing operations to perform for the frame. GetCurrentContext() Gets a pointer to the active, thread-local Clay layout context. public static extern Context* GetCurrentContext() Returns Context* A pointer to the current Context, or null if none is active. GetElementData(ElementId) Retrieves calculated layout details (such as the computed bounding box) for a specific element. public static extern ElementData GetElementData(ElementId id) Parameters id ElementId The unique ID of the element. Returns ElementData An ElementData structure containing layout metadata. GetElementId(ClayString) Computes a unique element ID from a descriptive ID string. public static extern ElementId GetElementId(ClayString idString) Parameters idString ClayString The string key identifying the element. Returns ElementId A unique ElementId for element declarations. GetElementIdWithIndex(ClayString, uint) Computes a unique element ID from a descriptive ID string and an index (e.g. inside loops). public static extern ElementId GetElementIdWithIndex(ClayString idString, uint index) Parameters idString ClayString The base string key identifying the collection. index uint An integer loop index to guarantee uniqueness. Returns ElementId A unique indexed ElementId. GetMaxElementCount() Gets the maximum number of UI elements that can be stored in the layout tree. public static extern int GetMaxElementCount() Returns int The maximum element capacity. GetMaxMeasureTextCacheWordCount() Gets the maximum capacity of the word cache used during text measurement. public static extern int GetMaxMeasureTextCacheWordCount() Returns int The word cache capacity. GetParentElementId() Retrieves the unique integer ID of the currently open parent element. public static extern uint GetParentElementId() Returns uint The parent element's ID value. GetPointerOverIds() Retrieves an array of all layout element IDs that the pointer is currently positioned over. public static extern ElementIdArray GetPointerOverIds() Returns ElementIdArray An array slice of ElementId structs representing the hovered hierarchy. GetScrollContainerData(ElementId) Retrieves scroll offset and container dimensions for a scrollable layout element. public static extern ScrollContainerData GetScrollContainerData(ElementId id) Parameters id ElementId The unique ID of the scroll container element. Returns ScrollContainerData A ScrollContainerData structure containing scroll state. GetScrollOffset() Gets the current active scroll offset accumulated for scroll containers. public static extern Vector2 GetScrollOffset() Returns Vector2 A Vector2 representing the horizontal and vertical scroll offsets. HashString(ClayString, uint, uint) Generates a unique layout ID based on a string key and hashing parameters. public static extern ElementId HashString(ClayString key, uint offset, uint seed) Parameters key ClayString The source string to hash. offset uint An offset index to append to avoid conflicts. seed uint A custom hashing seed value. Returns ElementId A hashed ElementId structure. Hovered() Checks if any element in the active layout was hovered by the pointer during the current frame. public static bool Hovered() Returns bool True if a layout element is currently hovered; otherwise, false. IsDebugModeEnabled() Checks whether Clay's debug mode is currently active. public static bool IsDebugModeEnabled() Returns bool True if debugging is enabled; otherwise, false. MinMemorySize() Retrieves the minimum size in bytes of the memory backing required for Clay's internal allocator. Call this before allocating memory for the arena. public static extern uint MinMemorySize() Returns uint The minimum memory size in bytes. OnHover(nint, nint) Registers a global hover callback that is invoked when any element configured to trigger on-hover events is hovered. public static extern void OnHover(nint onHoverFunction, nint userData) Parameters onHoverFunction nint A function pointer to a method matching the ClayNative.OnHoverCallback signature. userData nint A custom unmanaged data pointer that will be supplied to the callback. OpenElement() Low-level function: opens a new layout element scope. Must be paired with a corresponding call to CloseElement(). public static extern void OpenElement() OpenTextElement(ClayString, TextElementConfig*) Low-level function: opens and declares a text element within the current parent scope. public static extern void OpenTextElement(ClayString text, TextElementConfig* textConfig) Parameters text ClayString The string text to display. textConfig TextElementConfig* A pointer to the text rendering style parameters. PointerOver(ElementId) Determines whether the pointer is currently positioned over a specific layout element. public static bool PointerOver(ElementId elementId) Parameters elementId ElementId The unique ID of the element to check. Returns bool True if the pointer is hovering over the specified element; otherwise, false. RenderCommandArray_Get(RenderCommandArray, int) Helper: retrieves a pointer to a specific RenderCommand inside a RenderCommandArray at the specified index. public static extern RenderCommand* RenderCommandArray_Get(RenderCommandArray array, int index) Parameters array RenderCommandArray The list of render commands returned by EndLayout(). index int The 0-based index of the command to retrieve. Returns RenderCommand* A pointer to the render command structure, or null if the index is out of bounds. ResetMeasureTextCache() Resets the internal text measurement cache. Call this if fonts, sizes, or global zoom levels change. public static extern void ResetMeasureTextCache() SetCullingEnabled(bool) Enables or disables viewport culling. When enabled, elements outside the visible viewport are excluded from rendering. public static void SetCullingEnabled(bool enabled) Parameters enabled bool True to enable clipping/culling; otherwise, false. SetCurrentContext(Context*) Sets the active, thread-local Clay layout context. Useful for multi-context architectures where multiple layout states are maintained. public static extern void SetCurrentContext(Context* context) Parameters context Context* A pointer to the Context to set as active. SetDebugModeEnabled(bool) Enables or disables the native Clay debugger overlay. public static void SetDebugModeEnabled(bool enabled) Parameters enabled bool True to enable debug mode; otherwise, false. SetLayoutDimensions(Dimensions) Updates the dimensions of the layout viewport. Usually called on window resize. public static extern void SetLayoutDimensions(Dimensions dimensions) Parameters dimensions Dimensions The new viewport width and height. SetMaxElementCount(int) Sets the maximum number of UI elements that can be stored in the layout tree. Must be configured to match structural complexities. public static extern void SetMaxElementCount(int maxElementCount) Parameters maxElementCount int The new maximum element capacity. SetMaxMeasureTextCacheWordCount(int) Sets the maximum capacity of the word cache used during text measurement. public static extern void SetMaxMeasureTextCacheWordCount(int maxMeasureTextCacheWordCount) Parameters maxMeasureTextCacheWordCount int The new cache word limit. SetMeasureTextFunction(nint, void*) Configures the callback function that Clay will invoke whenever it needs to calculate text sizing. This callback must be provided if the layout tree contains any text elements. public static extern void SetMeasureTextFunction(nint measureTextFunction, void* userData) Parameters measureTextFunction nint A function pointer matching the ClayNative.Clay_MeasureTextCallback signature. userData void* A custom data pointer that will be supplied to the measurement callback. SetPointerState(Vector2, bool) Updates Clay's pointer input state (e.g. mouse or touch pointer coordinates and button state). public static void SetPointerState(Vector2 position, bool pointerDown) Parameters position Vector2 The current 2D screen coordinate of the pointer. pointerDown bool True if the primary pointer button is pressed; otherwise, false. SetQueryScrollOffsetFunction(nint, void*) Sets a custom callback to query scroll offsets if external scroll integration is desired. public static extern void SetQueryScrollOffsetFunction(nint queryScrollOffsetFunction, void* userData) Parameters queryScrollOffsetFunction nint A function pointer to the scroll querying function. userData void* A custom data pointer passed to the callback. StoreTextElementConfig(TextElementConfig) Low-level function: copies the text configuration into Clay's internal layout storage arena. public static extern TextElementConfig* StoreTextElementConfig(TextElementConfig config) Parameters config TextElementConfig The text configuration to store. Returns TextElementConfig* A pointer to the persisted config within native memory. UpdateScrollContainers(bool, Vector2, float) Processes scroll movements for scrollable containers based on user actions. public static void UpdateScrollContainers(bool enableDragScrolling, Vector2 scrollDelta, float deltaTime) Parameters enableDragScrolling bool If true, allows users to click and drag to scroll. scrollDelta Vector2 The scroll wheel or trackpad delta offset applied this frame. deltaTime float The time elapsed since the last frame in seconds.

Custom = 7 Applies a custom layout configuration or renderer for custom elements. Floating = 2 Allows the element to float dynamically relative to other elements (absolute positioning). api/ClaySharp.Interop.html.summary: Namespace ClaySharp.Interop Classes ClayNative Provides low-level, high-performance C# bindings to the native Clay layout library. Uses source-generated marshalling via LibraryImportAttribute and compiles with runtime marshalling disabled for zero-overhead, direct native transitions. Structs Arena Defines a contiguous block of pre-allocated unmanaged memory that Clay uses for all structural and dynamic layout allocations. AspectRatioElementConfig Imposes aspect ratio constraints on a UI element. AspectRatioElementConfigArray Unmanaged array wrapper for AspectRatioElementConfig elements. BorderElementConfig Configures styling properties for drawing borders around layout nodes. BorderElementConfigArray Unmanaged array wrapper for BorderElementConfig elements. BorderRenderData Describes calculated border coordinates and stroke styling emitted inside border render commands. BorderWidth Specifies border stroke widths for each edge of a container. BoundingBox Defines a 2D bounding rectangle representing computed UI element placements. ChildAlignment Specifies how child elements are aligned within their parent container along both axes. ClayString A native string wrapper used by Clay to represent text safely across the ABI. ClayStringSlice Represents a slice or segment of a string. Used by Clay's text layout engine during wrapping and text rendering. ClipElementConfig Configures boundary clipping properties for layout nodes. ClipElementConfigArray Unmanaged array wrapper for ClipElementConfig elements. ClipRenderData Describes clipping parameters emitted inside scissor/clip render commands. Color Represents a color using four floating-point components (Red, Green, Blue, Alpha). Context Opaque wrapper representing Clay's internal layout engine context context. This state should only be accessed via pointers returned by Clay_Initialize(Arena, Dimensions, ErrorHandler) or GetCurrentContext(). CornerRadius Specifies corner rounding radii for rectangles, borders, and images. CustomElementConfig Custom element layout configuration block. CustomElementConfigArray Unmanaged array wrapper for CustomElementConfig elements. CustomRenderData Describes a custom element payload emitted inside custom render commands. DebugElementData Structural debugging attributes associated with layout elements. Dimensions Represents standard 2D floating-point dimensions. ElementConfig Associates a layout configuration style with its category type identifier. ElementConfigArraySlice Represents a slice segment pointing into a native unmanaged array of ElementConfig entries. ElementConfigUnion Emulates an anonymous configuration C union inside ElementConfig, holding style detail pointers. Only one field should be accessed corresponding to the Type. ElementData Public structure containing calculated layout coordinates. ElementDeclaration Composite declaration structure encapsulating all layout, style, and rendering configs for a single UI element. Pass this to ConfigureOpenElement(ElementDeclaration) to apply styling. ElementId Holds unique identifiers generated by Clay to track element lifecycle, focus, and layout state. ElementIdArray Unmanaged array wrapper for ElementId elements. ErrorData Structure containing detailed diagnostic information returned when layout errors occur. ErrorHandler Holds callback function pointer registrations for native error handling. FloatingAttachPoints Defines target attachment points for floating elements. FloatingElementConfig Controls positioning, depth, and attachment behavior of floating (absolute positioned) UI elements. FloatingElementConfigArray Unmanaged array wrapper for FloatingElementConfig elements. ImageElementConfig Holds image element render configurations. ImageElementConfigArray Unmanaged array wrapper for ImageElementConfig elements. ImageRenderData Describes image content emitted inside image render commands. LayoutConfig Defines general layout configurations for element arrangement, spacing, and sizing. LayoutConfigArray Unmanaged array wrapper for LayoutConfig elements. LayoutElement Represents a core layout node within Clay's internal structural layout tree. LayoutElementChildren Emulates an anonymous C-style union containing either list of child element indices or text element details. Field offsets overlap so only one field should be accessed corresponding to layout node type. LayoutElementChildrenData Holds list of child elements inside a container. LayoutElementHashMapItem Represents an entry in Clay's internal layout spatial hash map, used for fast lookup, hover, and layout calculation. LayoutElementTreeNode A layout tree node structure tracking hierarchical rendering positioning. LayoutElementTreeRoot Defines the entry root configurations for a layout tree rendering hierarchy. MeasureTextCacheItem Represents an item stored inside Clay's internal text measurement cache. MeasuredWord Holds sizing metadata for a single word computed during text layout wrapping. Padding Defines inner spacing padding values surrounding the children of a container. PointerData Contains pointer (e.g. mouse or touch) position and state information. RectangleRenderData Describes a filled background rectangle emitted inside rectangle render commands. RenderCommand Encapsulates a single drawing instruction emitted by Clay's layout resolver. RenderCommandArray Contiguous unmanaged array containing the list of RenderCommand elements computed for the current frame. RenderData Emulates an anonymous C union packing specific layout drawing payloads. Only one field should be accessed corresponding to the CommandType. ScrollContainerData Public structure containing scroll coordinates and dimensions for scrollable elements. ScrollContainerDataInternal Internal layout engine scroll tracker holding physics and position metadata for a scroll container. SharedElementConfig Represents style settings that are shared across multiple layout nodes. SharedElementConfigArray Unmanaged array wrapper for SharedElementConfig elements. Sizing Defines layout sizing rules for both axes of a UI element. SizingAxis Specifies sizing rules and constraints for a single layout axis (either Width or Height). SizingAxisValue Emulates an anonymous C union representing a sizing constraint value. Field offsets overlap so that it can store either percentage values or min/max bounds. SizingMinMax Defines the minimum and maximum constraints for element dimensions. StringArray Unmanaged array wrapper for ClayString elements. TextElementConfig Provides configuration settings for rendering text elements. TextElementConfigArray Unmanaged array wrapper for TextElementConfig elements. TextElementData Holds layout and wrapping details computed specifically for text elements. TextRenderData Describes text content and style attributes emitted inside text render commands. Vector2 Represents a standard 2D vector. Warning Structure packing diagnostic warning messaging. WrappedTextLine Describes a single computed line of text resulting from word wrapping operations. WrappedTextLineArraySlice Represents a slice segment pointing into an unmanaged array of wrapped text lines. boolArray Unmanaged array wrapper for boolean elements. charArray Unmanaged array wrapper for raw character bytes. int32_tArray Unmanaged array wrapper for 32-bit signed integers. ErrorType Defines the specific errors that can occur during layout calculation or configuration in Clay. FloatingAttachPointType Defines the attachment points on an element and its target when using floating (absolute) positioning. FloatingAttachToElement Identifies which element a floating container attaches to. FloatingClipToElement Controls whether a floating element should be clipped by another element's bounds. LayoutAlignmentX Controls horizontal alignment of children within a layout container. LayoutAlignmentY Controls vertical alignment of children within a layout container. LayoutDirection Defines the layout direction along which children are arranged. PointerCaptureMode Controls pointer capture behavior for floating elements. PointerDataInteractionState Represents the interaction state of a pointer (e.g. mouse or touch) on a UI element. getting-started.html.title: Getting Started with ClaySharp | BlackHook
getting-started.html.summary: Getting Started with ClaySharp This guide walks you through the step-by-step process of integrating ClaySharp into your C# rendering pipeline. 📋 Integration Steps Step 1: Memory Arena Allocation Clay runs within a single block of unmanaged memory (a linear arena). You must query the engine for its minimum allocation size, allocate a backing block, and initialize the Arena structure: using System.Runtime.InteropServices; using ClaySharp.Interop; // 1. Query minimum size required by the layout engine uint minMemoryBytes = ClayNative.MinMemorySize(); // 2. Allocate the unmanaged backing memory block void* arenaMemory = (void*)Marshal.AllocHGlobal((int)minMemoryBytes); // 3. Create the Arena structure Arena arena = ClayNative.CreateArenaWithCapacityAndMemory((UIntPtr)minMemoryBytes, arenaMemory); Step 2: Initialize Context Initialize Clay with the memory arena, the initial screen dimensions, and a diagnostic error handler: var dimensions = new Dimensions { Width = 1024, Height = 768 }; var errorHandler = new ErrorHandler { ErrorHandlerFunction = Marshal.GetFunctionPointerForDelegate(new Action<ErrorData>(OnClayError)), UserData = null }; // Initialize the native layout context ClayNative.Clay_Initialize(arena, dimensions, errorHandler); // --- static void OnClayError(ErrorData error) { string msg = Marshal.PtrToStringAnsi((IntPtr)error.ErrorText.Chars, error.ErrorText.Length); Console.WriteLine($"[Clay Layout Error] {error.ErrorType}: {msg}"); } Step 3: Register Text Measurement Callback If your user interface displays text elements, Clay must be able to calculate text dimensions. Register a callback that implements your custom text measurement algorithm: // Define the measurement delegate var measureCallback = new ClayNative.Clay_MeasureTextCallback(MeasureText); // Register the callback with Clay ClayNative.SetMeasureTextFunction(Marshal.GetFunctionPointerForDelegate(measureCallback), null); // --- static Dimensions MeasureText(ClayStringSlice text, TextElementConfig* config, void* userData) { // Access styling using config->FontSize, config->FontId, etc. float width = text.Length * (config->FontSize * 0.5f); // Simple fallback width approximation return new Dimensions { Width = width, Height = config->FontSize }; } Step 4: The Frame Layout Loop During your application update and render frames, feed pointer inputs and execute a layout calculations block. You can declare layout elements using C# resource scopes (using blocks): public static unsafe void RunLayoutFrame(float mouseX, float mouseY, bool isLeftMouseDown) { // A. Feed pointer position and button state to Clay ClayNative.SetPointerState(new Vector2 { X = mouseX, Y = mouseY }, isLeftMouseDown); // B. Begin layout pass calculation ClayNative.BeginLayout(); // Declare a root panel declaration var rootPanel = new ElementDeclaration { Id = ClayNative.GetElementId(CreateClayString("root_panel")), BackgroundColor = new Color { R = 20, G = 20, B = 22, A = 255 }, Layout = new LayoutConfig { LayoutDirection = LayoutDirection.LeftToRight, ChildAlignment = new ChildAlignment { X = LayoutAlignmentX.Center, Y = LayoutAlignmentY.Center }, Sizing = new Sizing { Width = new SizingAxis { Type = SizingType.Grow }, Height = new SizingAxis { Type = SizingType.Grow } } } }; // C. Declare the UI structure using (new ClayElementScope(in rootPanel)) { // Define children here... } // D. Finalize calculations and get the output draw commands RenderCommandArray commands = ClayNative.EndLayout(); // E. Paint the UI using the commands RenderCommands(commands); } Step 5: Processing Render Commands Iterate through the resulting RenderCommandArray and draw each item using your graphics backend (e.g. SkiaSharp, OpenGL, Vulkan): static void RenderCommands(RenderCommandArray commands) { for (int i = 0; i < commands.Length; i++) { RenderCommand* cmd = ClayNative.RenderCommandArray_Get(commands, i); if (cmd == null) continue; switch (cmd->CommandType) { case RenderCommandType.Rectangle: // Draw filled rectangle using cmd->BoundingBox and cmd->RenderData.Rectangle break; case RenderCommandType.Text: // Paint text using cmd->BoundingBox and cmd->RenderData.Text break; case RenderCommandType.ScissorStart: // Clip drawing coordinates to cmd->BoundingBox area break; case RenderCommandType.ScissorEnd: // Restore previous clipping boundaries break; } } }

index.html.title: BlackHook | BlackHook
index.html.summary: BlackHook Welcome to the BlackHook & ClaySharp developer documentation portal. BlackHook is a high-performance native desktop shell windowing target built using modern .NET and native rendering components. At its core, it leverages ClaySharp, an advanced C# binding to the Clay high-performance 2D layout engine. 🎯 What is ClaySharp? ClaySharp provides modern, zero-overhead C# bindings to Clay, enabling developers to build complex, responsive, dynamic layout trees with native performance. By utilizing compile-time source-generated marshalling and disabling runtime marshalling globally, ClaySharp operates with zero garbage collection allocations in its layout loop. 📚 Explore the Documentation To get started with BlackHook and ClaySharp, explore the following resources: Introduction Learn about the core design, architecture, and feature set of the layout engine. Getting Started Guide Walk through memory arena allocations, text measurement registrations, and running your first layout frame. API Reference Dive into the generated metadata, enums, structures, and low-level source-generated interop methods.

introduction.html.title: Introduction to ClaySharp | BlackHook
introduction.html.summary: Introduction to ClaySharp ClaySharp is a modern C# binding for Clay, a high-performance 2D layout engine written as a single-header C library. Designed for game engines, desktop shells (such as BlackHook), and graphics-intensive applications, ClaySharp allows C# developers to define complex, responsive user interfaces with absolute minimum overhead. 🚀 Key Features Zero-Allocation Layout Loop: Designed from the ground up to prevent heap allocation churn. Dynamic layout trees are computed inside an unmanaged memory block, bypassing C# Garbage Collection completely. Flexbox-like Alignment Rules: Easily position children horizontally or vertically with customized alignment (left/top, center, right/bottom) and layout spacing gap sizes. Custom Sizing & Aspect Ratios: Supports flexible sizing constraints including fixed sizing (Fixed), matching children bounds (Fit), proportioning sibling layouts (Grow), or scaling relative to parents (Percent), alongside explicit aspect ratio locks. Floating (Absolute) Coordinates: Design menus, tooltips, dialogs, and panels that float relative to parents, specific elements by ID, or the viewport root with dedicated depth (ZIndex) layers. Viewport Culling & Clipping: Enable boundary clipping and culling elements outside the visible viewport to optimize rendering performance for lists or nested views. Pointer Hover & Scroll Handling: Seamlessly update mouse/touch inputs and process viewport scrolling offsets, momentum, and click collisions natively. 🏛 Architecture & Performance Design Unlike traditional UI frameworks that utilize deep inheritance trees, reflection, or heavy managed wrappers, ClaySharp focuses on raw ABI compatibility and ultra-fast transitions: ┌─────────────────┐ [LibraryImport] ┌──────────────┐ │ C# Application │ ──────────────────────────► │ Clay Native │ │ (Managed code) │ ◄────────────────────────── │ (C library) │ └─────────────────┘ Disable Marshalling └──────────────┘ Source-Generated Marshalling ([LibraryImport]): By shifting native marshalling code generation to compile time, C# compiles optimized transition instructions, avoiding reflection overhead entirely. Runtime Marshalling Disabled: We disable runtime marshalling via [assembly: DisableRuntimeMarshalling], ensuring C# structures are passed direct bit-for-bit (blitted) without conversions. No Garbage Collection Transitions: High-frequency APIs are marked with [SuppressGCTransition] to prevent GC overhead on every layout query.
